content:
# Hive 1 - inspection log

## 2026-03-15
- Queen: present, laying. Spotted her on frame 4.
- Brood: solid pattern, 6 frames.
- Stores: 2 frames capped honey, light. Fed 1:1 syrup.
- Varroa: sugar roll 2 per 100. Below threshold.
- Temperament: calm.

## 2026-02-10
- Quick winter check. Cluster strong, fondant added.

## 2025-11-09
- Wrapped for winter. Mouse guard on.

# Hive 2 - inspection log

## 2026-03-15
- Queen: present, good laying. Brood across 7 frames.
- Brood: tight pattern, lots of eggs.
- Stores: heavy, 4 frames capped. No feed needed.
- Varroa: sugar roll 1 per 100. Clean.
- Temperament: calm. Best behaved of the row.
- Note: strong colony. Candidate for a split come April.

## 2026-01-20
- Hefted from back. Heavy. Cluster up top.

# Hive 3 - inspection log

## 2026-03-08
- Queen: not found. No eggs, no young larvae.
- Brood: spotty old capped brood only. Drone layer suspect or queenless.
- Stores: adequate, 3 frames.
- Varroa: sugar roll 4 per 100. Watch.
- Temperament: agitated, roaring when opened.
- Action: queenless. Order mated queen for early spring. Combine if no improvement.

## 2026-02-08
- Light cluster. Concerned.

# Hive 4 - inspection log

## 2026-03-08
- Queen: present, laying. Marked queen, white dot.
- Brood: 5 frames, good pattern.
- Stores: moderate. Topped fondant.
- Varroa: sugar roll 3 per 100. Borderline.
- Temperament: calm.

## 2025-12-02
- Final fall check. Cluster size good.

# Hive 5 - inspection log

## 2026-02-22
- Queen: present. Saw eggs, did not spot her.
- Brood: building, 4 frames.
- Stores: low. Fed fondant and 1:1 once temps allow.
- Varroa: sugar roll 6 per 100. Over threshold.
- Temperament: calm.
- Action: oxalic acid dribble done 2026-02-22. Recheck in two weeks.

## 2026-01-15
- Cluster small but alive. Worried about stores.

## 2025-10-30
- Treated with formic strips end of season.

# Hive 6 - inspection log

## 2026-02-22
- Queen: present, laying well for the date.
- Brood: 5 frames, solid.
- Stores: good.
- Varroa: sugar roll 2 per 100. Clean.
- Temperament: calm.

## 2025-11-18
- Wrapped. Quilt box on for moisture.

# Hive 7 - inspection log

## 2026-03-15
- Queen: present, laying. Big spring buildup.
- Brood: 8 frames, edge to edge.
- Stores: heavy.
- Varroa: sugar roll 3 per 100.
- Temperament: calm but busy.
- Note: needs a second box soon. Reverse boxes next visit. Swarm risk if I sit on it.

## 2026-02-12
- Strongest cluster in the yard.

# Hive 8 - inspection log

## 2026-02-10
- Queen: present, marginal laying. Older queen, second year.
- Brood: 3 frames, pattern getting shotgun.
- Stores: adequate.
- Varroa: sugar roll 5 per 100. Treat.
- Temperament: defensive.
- Action: plan to requeen this spring. Pattern failing.

## 2025-12-09
- Hefted light. Added fondant.

# Hive 9 - inspection log

## 2026-03-01
- Queen: present, laying. Found her on frame 3.
- Brood: 6 frames, good pattern.
- Stores: moderate, fed 1:1.
- Varroa: sugar roll 2 per 100.
- Temperament: calm.
- Note: swap to drawn comb for buildup. Good honey producer last year, 55 lbs.

## 2026-01-28
- Cluster active on warm day. Cleansing flights.

# Hive 10 - inspection log

## 2026-03-01
- Queen: present, laying.
- Brood: 5 frames.
- Stores: low. Fed fondant.
- Varroa: sugar roll 4 per 100. Borderline.
- Temperament: calm.

## 2025-11-22
- Combined weak nuc into this one in fall. Holding.

# Hive 11 - inspection log

## 2026-03-22
- Queen: present, strong laying. New queen from last summer.
- Brood: 7 frames, tight wall to wall.
- Stores: good.
- Varroa: sugar roll 1 per 100. Clean.
- Temperament: very calm. Gentle line.
- Note: best candidate for grafting larvae. Use for queen rearing.

## 2026-02-18
- Cluster large. Already bringing pollen.

# Hive 12 - inspection log

## 2026-03-22
- Queen: present, laying. Spotted eggs and young larvae.
- Brood: 5 frames, improving.
- Stores: moderate.
- Varroa: sugar roll 3 per 100.
- Temperament: calm.
- Note: slow starter. Watch buildup before main flow.

## 2026-01-30
- Alive, small cluster. Fondant on.

# Wintering checklist

Fox Chase yard. Done by late November.

- Heft each hive. Light ones get fondant, not syrup, once cold.
- Mouse guards on all 12.
- Top entrance open for ventilation.
- Tilt hives forward so condensation runs off the inner cover.
- Quilt box or moisture board on top.
- Wind break on north side.
- Final mite count before wrapping. Treat with oxalic on broodless cluster if over threshold.
- Strap lids against wind.

# Varroa treatment plan

Monitor by sugar roll, 300 bees. Threshold 3 per 100 in spring, 2 per 100 going into fall.

## Schedule
- Late winter: oxalic acid dribble while broodless. Done on hives 3 and 5.
- Spring: monitor monthly. Drone comb trapping on strong hives.
- Late summer after harvest: formic acid strips, temperature permitting.
- Fall: oxalic vapor or dribble on the broodless cluster.

Rotate chemistry. Do not lean on one treatment. Record counts per hive in inspection logs.

# Spring harvest 2026

Pulled late May into early June. Locust and tulip poplar flow.

## Totals
- Frames pulled: 96.
- Extracted: about 310 lbs.
- Best producers: hives 2, 7, 9, 11.
- Light producers: hives 3, 5, 8. Left their supers.

## Notes
- Moisture under 18 percent on the refractometer. Good.
- Bottled 240 lbs for market. Rest in bulk pails.
- Combined with late-summer pull this puts the year near 600 lbs.

# SE PA Beekeepers Guild notes

Southeastern Pennsylvania Beekeepers Guild.

## Meeting takeaways
- Mite loads ran high across the region last fall. Treat early.
- Local nuc orders open in February. Place early for spring pickup.
- Guild bulk order on woodenware and jars cuts cost. Sign up by January.
- Speaker covered oxalic acid vapor safety. Respirator, no open flame.

## To do
- Submit hive count for the apiary registry.
- Bring honey for the guild fair tasting table.

# Queen rearing notes

Goal: raise 6 to 8 queens from the gentle line in hive 11.

## Method
- Graft day old larvae from hive 11.
- Cell builder: strong queenless starter, packed with nurse bees.
- Day 10 move cells to mating nucs.
- Mating nucs in the back row, away from the main flight paths.

## Timing
- Start grafts once drones are flying and weather holds.
- Check for laying queens about three weeks after the cells go in.

Keep records of which graft batch goes to which nuc.

# Honey sales - Briarwood Farmers Market

Sell raw honey at Briarwood Farmers Market on weekends.

## Pack
- 1 lb jars and 8 oz jars.
- Comb honey when I have it. Sells fast.
- Label with apiary name and harvest season.

## Pricing
- 1 lb jar holds steady. 8 oz for the smaller buyers.
- Bulk pails to two regular buyers.

## Notes
- Spring honey lighter, sells first.
- Late-summer honey darker, stronger. Repeat buyers prefer it.

# Swarm prevention

Strong hives in spring will swarm if I sit on them.

## Checks
- Look for queen cells along the bottom and edges of frames.
- Watch for backfilling of the brood nest with nectar.
- Crowded brood box means act now.

## Actions
- Reverse brood boxes early spring.
- Add space ahead of the flow, do not wait.
- Split the strongest. Hives 2 and 7 are first in line.
- Cut queen cells only if I am also giving space. Cutting alone does not work.

# Feeding syrup ratios

## 1:1 sugar to water
- Spring buildup feed. Stimulates laying.
- Use on light hives once temps stay above freezing.

## 2:1 sugar to water
- Fall feed to build winter stores.
- Heavier, bees store it fast.

## Fondant
- Winter emergency feed. Goes right on the top bars.
- Use when too cold for liquid.

Never feed syrup with honey supers on. Mix clean, no additives.

# Late-summer harvest 2025

Pulled mid August. Knotweed and goldenrod flow.

## Totals
- Extracted: about 295 lbs.
- Darker honey, strong taste.
- Best producers: hives 7, 9, 11.

## Notes
- Goldenrod smells sharp in the honey house. Normal.
- Left enough on every hive for winter. Did not strip them.
- Spring plus late-summer for the year came to roughly 600 lbs.
- Started fall mite treatment right after pulling supers.

# Woodenware and equipment

## On hand
- Deep boxes and mediums, mix of both. Standardizing on mediums over time.
- Spare frames with foundation, ready to draw.
- Two extra bottom boards and inner covers.

## To build or buy
- Six medium supers before spring flow.
- More frames. Always short on frames.
- New smoker fuel, burlap and pine needles.

## Maintenance
- Scrape and torch any boxes from dead outs before reuse.
- Repaint lids over winter.

- Brood: 6 frames solid.

- Temperament: calm.
